The Ant Adventures

Vietnam, thoughtfully arranged — small groups, families, and fully tailor-made journeys, never mass tourism. One complete reference: who we are, what we do, and seven sample tours in full detail.

Culture & Values Who We Serve Services Hotels Local Experts Sample Tours

Culture & Core Values

What guides every itinerary we build, whether it's a first-time leisure trip or a recurring business account.

Authentic Experience

Every itinerary is built around something a guest can't easily book online — a private access point, an unusual local connection, or a genuinely uncommon combination of places.

Quiet, Attentive Care

Service that notices the small things without announcing itself — the brand voice is thoughtful and sensory, never scripted or generic.

Deep Local Connection

Relationships with artisans, guides, hoteliers, and suppliers built over years across North, Central, and South Vietnam — not a directory of vendors.

Discretion & Reliability

For business and VIP guests especially, the differentiator is seamless logistics and confidentiality, not novelty.

Small Group, Never Mass Tourism

Trips are built for intimate groups, families, and fully tailor-made solo or couple travel — not fixed-departure coach buses. Group sizes are kept deliberately small so guests stay close to the place, the people, and the pace, not the crowd.

Who We Serve

Thomas's client base spans four profiles — each shapes how we design and deliver a trip.

Slow-Luxury Culture & Cuisine Travelers

Affluent leisure travelers wanting immersive, story-driven experiences — private chef tables, artisan visits, off-schedule cultural access.

Adventure & Active Travelers

Trekking, cycling, kayaking, and other physically engaging itineraries, still at a premium comfort level.

Family & Multi-Generational Groups

Itineraries balancing comfort, downtime, and variety across ages.

Business Travelers

Full-service concierge for guests visiting Vietnam for work — transport, accommodation, interpreter support, and business-appropriate dining, with leisure as an optional add-on.

What We Can Do For You

Beyond itineraries — how support flows from the moment a guest is booked to the moment they fly home.

01Before You Arrive

  • Custom Tour Design

    From half-day city experiences to 21-day grand circuits, built around the guest's interests and pace.

  • Visa Arrangement

    Entry visa processing handled ahead of arrival, so guests land ready to go.

02On Arrival

  • Fast-Track Immigration

    Meet-and-greet at the aircraft door and expedited immigration/customs at major airports.

  • Private Transportation

    Dedicated vehicles and drivers, domestic flights, and train bookings including luxury and vintage carriages.

03During Your Trip

  • Hotel & Resort Bookings

    5-star hotels, boutique properties, and private villas across all three regions.

  • Luxury Cruises

    Halong Bay, Lan Ha Bay, and Mekong River cruise bookings, cabin class matched to budget.

  • Guides & Interpreters

    English-speaking national guides, local specialists, and interpreters for any language on request.

  • Restaurant & Private Dining

    Reservations at top restaurants, private chef tables, and business dining arrangements.

  • Wellness & Special Requests

    Spa bookings, dietary accommodations, accessibility needs, and celebration add-ons.

04Always Available

  • Security & Protocol Escort

    Discreet close-protection or protocol escort available for VIP and high-profile guests.

  • Special Arrangements on Request

    Private jet charters, helicopter transfers or scenic flights, and private yacht/cruise charters — arranged for guests who want an extra layer of exclusivity or need to move quickly between regions.

Hotels We Work With

A working shortlist of 4- and 5-star properties by region — confirm current rates and room availability before quoting.

Hanoi

  • Sofitel Legend Metropole Hanoi5-Star
    Colonial landmark in the heart of the Old Quarter
  • Capella Hanoi5-Star
    Opera-inspired design steps from the Opera House

    Boutique, personal service, central location

    Modern flagship near the convention centre

    Overwater pavilions on West Lake

Central Vietnam (Hoi An / Da Nang / Hue)

    Beachfront pool villas south of Hoi An

    Boutique riverside resort

    Beachfront, family-friendly

    Iconic hillside resort on Son Tra Peninsula

    Art Deco riverside former Governor's residence

    Ultra-luxury in the Times Square Building

    Art Deco boutique in District 1

    Historic landmark on Lam Son Square

    Riverside, business district

Phu Quoc

  • Cassia Cottage4-Star
    Boutique beachfront on Ong Lang Beach

    Whimsical design resort, Emerald Bay

    Long Beach, family & couples

Con Dao

Mekong Delta

  • Legacy Mekong, Cai Be4-Star
    Riverside boutique heritage estate
  • Azerai Can Tho5-Star
    Riverside resort in Can Tho

    Colonial-style riverfront hotel

Nha Trang

  • Six Senses Ninh Van Bay5-Star
    Private bay, villa-only, boat access
  • InterContinental Nha Trang5-Star
    Beachfront city resort
  • Amiana Resort Nha Trang5-Star

Quy Nhon

  • Zannier Hotels Bai San Ho5-Star
    Secluded cove, ultra-luxury

    Private peninsula villas

    Beachfront, more accessible tier
This is a working shortlist, not the full list — we work with many other hotels in every region, and always stay flexible to match the specific property, room type, and budget a guest requests.

Working With Local Experts

Every itinerary is built alongside people who know the ground better than any guidebook — village elders in the northern highlands, boat families on the Mekong, artisans in Hoi An's workshops, and national park rangers in Cat Tien. Working directly with local experts means guests get access that can't be booked online, and it means tourism income reaches the communities hosting the trip, not just the hotels and coach operators. This is a standing commitment across every region we operate in, not a one-off add-on.

Sample Tours — Full Day-by-Day Detail

Seven starting points — five fixed itineraries, one tailor-made framework, and one business travel template.

Tour 1Vietnam Grand Journey Tour 2Vietnam Panorama Tour 3North Vietnam: Sapa by Luxury Train Tour 4Central Vietnam by Vintage Train Tour 5Saigon, Mekong & Cambodia by Speedboat Tour 6Tailor-Made Journey Tour 7Business Travel & Concierge
Tour 1

Vietnam Grand Journey

The Classic Route, North to South — 5-Star Collection

Duration14 Days / 13 Nights
RouteHanoi → Halong Bay → Ninh Binh → Hue → Da Nang/Hoi An → Saigon → Mekong Delta
PaceRelaxed — classic sightseeing, private guided
Accommodation5-star hotels & luxury cruise throughout
Day Itinerary Brief Introduction & Explanation Photo
D1 Arrive Hanoi Private transfer into the Old Quarter as dusk settles over Hoan Kiem Lake. A welcome dinner introduces the quiet rhythm of the capital before the journey south begins.
D2 Hanoi City in Full Temple of Literature, the French Quarter, and the ceremony of egg coffee poured slow. Vietnam's thousand-year capital, read at walking pace.
D3 Halong Bay – Embark Overnight Cruise Limestone karsts rise straight from the water as a private cruise casts off. Evening squid-fishing on deck, the bay gone silver under the moon.
D4 Halong Bay – Cruise & Disembark Sunrise tai chi on the sundeck, a cave visit by rowboat, then back to shore and on toward the limestone valleys of Ninh Binh.
D5 Ninh Binh – Trang An & Mua Cave A sampan drifts through flooded caves beneath towering karst — Halong Bay's inland twin. Those who climb Mua Cave's steps are rewarded with the valley laid out below.
D6 Fly to Hue – Imperial Citadel The old royal capital, walled and moated, its Forbidden Purple City still holding the hush of dynasties past.
D7 Hue to Hoi An via Hai Van Pass The coastal road climbs into cloud at Hai Van Pass — one of the great drives of Vietnam — before the Golden Bridge's stone hands at Ba Na Hills and on to Hoi An.
D8 Hoi An Ancient Town Lantern-lit lanes, tailor ateliers, and a town that has traded quietly with the world since the 15th century.
D9 Coconut Village – Fly to Saigon A basket-boat spin through the palm-lined waterways of Cam Thanh, then south by air into the neon hum of Ho Chi Minh City.
D10 Saigon City Tour Notre-Dame Cathedral, the Reunification Palace, and Ben Thanh Market's tangle of scent and colour — old Saigon and new, side by side.
D11 Cu Chi Tunnels & Saigon by Night A morning underground in the wartime tunnel network, an evening above ground for a street-food crawl through Saigon's night markets.
D12 Mekong Delta Day Trip Floating markets, fruit orchards, and villages that live by the tide — a full day on the water in Vietnam's rice basket.
D13 Free Day & Farewell Dinner A day left open — rooftop bar, spa, or one last wander — closing with a farewell dinner over the Saigon River skyline.
D14 Departure Final souvenirs at Ben Thanh Market, then private transfer to the airport.
Tour 2

Vietnam Panorama

Classic Route + Trekking & Nature, North to South

Duration14 Days / 13 Nights
RouteHanoi → Sapa → Halong Bay → Ninh Binh → Hue → Hoi An → Saigon → Cat Tien
PaceActive — trekking days combined with classic sightseeing
Accommodation4–5-star hotels, mountain lodges & luxury cruise
Day Itinerary Brief Introduction & Explanation Photo
D1 Arrive Hanoi The Old Quarter's motorbike hum and lakeside calm — first steps into Vietnam before the mountains call.
D2 Hanoi City Tour The Opera House, French colonial streets, and a city that wears its history in layers.
D3 Overnight Train to Sapa An evening sleeper train climbs north into the mountains, cabins rocking gently toward Lao Cai.
D4 Sapa Trek – Cat Cat & Muong Hoa Valley Terraced rice fields cut into near-vertical hillsides, H'mong villages along the trail, the Hoang Lien Son range on the horizon.
D5 Sapa Trek – Ta Van & Giang Ta Chai Deeper into the valley on foot, past waterwheels and homestays, ending with tea in a hillside village.
D6 Return to Hanoi – Halong Bay Cruise Back down from the mountains and straight out to sea — a private cruise casts off into Halong Bay's karst maze by afternoon.
D7 Halong Bay Kayak & Ninh Binh Morning kayak through hidden lagoons, then transfer inland to the limestone valleys of Ninh Binh.
D8 Ninh Binh – Trang An, Mua Cave & Cycling A sampan through flooded caves, a climb to Mua Cave's viewpoint, and an easy bike ride through rice-paddy villages.
D9 Fly to Hue – Imperial Citadel The walled royal city and a dragon boat drift on the Perfume River at day's end.
D10 Hue to Hoi An via Hai Van Pass (Cycling Option) The coast road over Hai Van Pass, cycling optional on the descent, then on to Hoi An's lantern light.
D11 Hoi An Ancient Town & Cham Islands A boat out to the Cham Islands for snorkeling and quiet beaches, or a slower day exploring Hoi An's tailor shops and temples.
D12 Fly to Saigon The Saigon River lit up at dusk — the south's fast pulse after the mountains and coast.
D13 Cat Tien National Park – Jungle Trek A day trek through primary rainforest, tracking gibbons and hornbills with a park naturalist.
D14 Departure A final coffee near the Central Post Office before the airport transfer.
Tour 3

North Vietnam: Sapa by Luxury Train

Trekking & Nature Focus, Mountain North

Duration7 Days / 6 Nights (adjustable)
RouteHanoi → Sapa (luxury overnight train) → road transfer back to Hanoi
PaceActive — 2–3 days of guided trekking
Accommodation5-star Hanoi hotel + luxury mountain lodge, private luxury train cabin
Day Itinerary Brief Introduction & Explanation Photo
D1 Arrive Hanoi Old Quarter welcome dinner, a first taste of the capital before heading for the hills.
D2 Hanoi Highlights – Board the Luxury Night Train A day in the capital, then an evening boarding of the restored luxury sleeper carriage north to Lao Cai — a slower, more romantic way into the mountains.
D3 Arrive Sapa – Cat Cat Village Trek Morning arrival in Lao Cai, drive up to Sapa, and an easy first trek through Cat Cat's waterfalls and H'mong houses.
D4 Full-Day Trek – Ta Van, Lao Chai & Giang Ta Chai The valley's full circuit on foot — rice terraces, suspension bridges, and lunch in a Dao village home.
D5 Fansipan or Silver Waterfall A choice of days: the cable car to Fansipan's summit, the 'Roof of Indochina', or a gentler visit to Silver Waterfall and Tram Ton Pass.
D6 Scenic Road Transfer Back to Hanoi A private car winds back down through the mountains, the terraces and passes one last time before the flat delta returns.
D7 Departure Free morning in Hanoi, then transfer to the airport.
Tour 4

Central Vietnam by Vintage Train

Hue – Da Nang – Hoi An – Quy Nhon

Duration7 Days / 6 Nights (adjustable)
RouteHue → Da Nang → Hoi An → Quy Nhon (vintage train)
PaceRelaxed — heritage, coast & one scenic rail journey
Accommodation5-star hotels & resorts, private vintage train carriage
Day Itinerary Brief Introduction & Explanation Photo
D1 Arrive Hue Transfer into the old imperial capital, its citadel walls quiet against the evening light.
D2 Hue – Perfume River & Royal Tombs A dragon boat drifts the Perfume River to Thien Mu Pagoda, then on to the moss-covered royal tombs outside the city.
D3 Hue to Hoi An via Hai Van Pass One of Vietnam's great coastal drives, cloud-wrapped Hai Van Pass, then the Golden Bridge at Ba Na Hills en route to Hoi An.
D4 Hoi An Ancient Town Lantern-lit lanes by night, tailor shops and temples by day — a trading port frozen in amber light.
D5 Board the Vintage Train to Quy Nhon Back to Da Nang to board a beautifully restored vintage carriage — velvet seats and open windows for a slow, scenic ride down the coast to Quy Nhon.
D6 Quy Nhon – Ky Co & Eo Gio Hidden coves, turquoise water, and a coastline still largely undiscovered by mass tourism.
D7 Quy Nhon Leisure & Departure A final slow morning by the beach before transfer to the airport.
Tour 5

Saigon, Mekong & Cambodia by Speedboat

South Vietnam Focus

Duration5 Days / 4 Nights
RouteSaigon → Cu Chi → Mekong Delta (3 days) → Speedboat to Phnom Penh, Cambodia
PaceRelaxed — city, tunnels & river
Accommodation5-star Saigon hotel + boutique Mekong riverside stays
Day Itinerary Brief Introduction & Explanation Photo
D1 Arrive Saigon – City Tour Notre-Dame Cathedral, the Reunification Palace, and Ben Thanh Market's tangle of scent and colour on the first afternoon.
D2 Cu Chi Tunnels & Saigon by Night A morning underground in the wartime tunnel network, an evening street-food crawl through the city's night markets.
D3 Mekong Delta Day 1 – Cai Be & Vinh Long Into the delta by road and boat — floating markets, fruit orchards, and villages that live by the tide, overnighting on the riverside.
D4 Mekong Delta Day 2 – Can Tho & Chau Doc A sunrise paddle through Can Tho's floating market, then on to Chau Doc at the edge of the delta, gateway to Cambodia.
D5 Mekong Delta Day 3 – Tra Su Forest & Speedboat to Phnom Penh A gentle paddle through Tra Su's flooded melaleuca forest at dawn, then a speedboat cruise up the Mekong/Bassac River, crossing the border into Cambodia and arriving in Phnom Penh by afternoon.
Tour 6

Tailor-Made Journey

Fully Customized to Your Guest's Request

DurationFlexible — 3 to 21+ days
RouteAny combination of North, Central & South Vietnam
PaceSet entirely by the guest
AccommodationMatched to guest preference, from boutique to ultra-luxury
Note: This is not a fixed itinerary — it's a framework. Every element below can be added, removed, or reshaped around the guest's dates, interests, and budget. Use Tours 1–5 as a starting canvas, then swap in any of the building blocks below.
Block Itinerary Brief Introduction & Explanation Photo
B1 Bespoke Accommodation Private pool villas, boutique 5-star boutique hotels, or a suite on a luxury Halong Bay cruise — matched to the guest's taste rather than a fixed category.
B2 Curated Experiences Private chef tables, artisan workshops, off-schedule temple access, trekking, cycling, diving, or a slow afternoon doing nothing at all.
B3 Flexible Pace & Duration A 3-day city escape or a 21-day grand circuit — the calendar bends to the guest, not the other way round.
B4 Private Transport & Guides Dedicated vehicle and driver throughout, an English-speaking guide, and an interpreter on request for any language.
B5 Special Requests Celebrations, dietary needs, accessibility, photography add-ons, wellness routines — flagged in advance and quietly handled.
Tour 7

Business Travel & Concierge

Factory Visits, Negotiations & Contract Signing — We Handle Everything Else

DurationSample 5 Days / 4 Nights (scales to full trip length)
RouteAny city or industrial zone — sample below shows Saigon
PaceBuilt entirely around the business agenda; leisure is optional add-on
Accommodation5-star business hotel of guest's choice
Note: Visa arranged in advance, dedicated account manager assigned for the full trip, and a professional interpreter accompanies the guest through every factory visit, negotiation, and signing — not just available on request. Coordination with the guest's local partner/factory confirms the agenda before arrival, and a discreet security/protocol escort can be added for VIP or high-profile guests. Leisure elements are optional add-ons scheduled only around confirmed meeting and site-visit times.
Day Itinerary Brief Introduction & Explanation Photo
D1 VIP Arrival & Fast-Track Meet-and-greet at the aircraft door, fast-track immigration and baggage, then a private transfer to the hotel with SIM/Wi-Fi already set up. The assigned interpreter joins for a same-day briefing on the week's factory visits and meetings.
D2 Factory / Production Site Visit Private car and dedicated interpreter escort the guest to the partner factory or production facility, sitting in on technical discussions and quality inspections, followed by a working lunch with the local team.
D3 Negotiation & Contract Signing A full day dedicated to commercial negotiation, with the interpreter at the table throughout; document translation support and signing-ceremony coordination for the final contract, closing with a business dinner to mark the agreement.
D4 Second Site Visit or Optional Leisure Half-Day A further factory/supplier visit if the agenda calls for it, or a half-day Cu Chi Tunnels or city highlights add-on for downtime, closing with a reserved table for an evening business dinner.
D5 Departure Fast-track check-in and immigration, lounge access, and a private transfer to the airport.
